ks1.objective: React quickly to the coach's call and race to safety before being tagged.
successCriteria: Listens carefully for the correct call

lks2.objective: Develop reaction speed and agility whilst making quick decisions under pressure.
successCriteria: Reacts immediately to the coach's signal
Escapes using speed and changes of direction

uks2.objective: React, accelerate and compete in high-tempo chase situations.

space.description: Set up a rectangular playing area with a central line dividing two teams. Players stand opposite a partner either side of the line. End lines act as safety zones.

value.name: Bravery
value.text: Players must react quickly, commit to decisions and keep trying even when they are tagged.

steps: Split players into two teams: England and Scotland.
Players stand opposite a partner across the central line.
The called team races towards their safety line.
The opposite team attempts to tag them before they escape.
progressions: Use similar sounding words to increase concentration.
Start from different body positions such as kneeling, sitting or lying down.
Add a football and require players to dribble whilst escaping.
Complete a ball mastery skill before reacting.
questions: How can you make it harder for your opponent to tag you?
What body position helps you accelerate fastest?

step.space: Reduce the distance to increase reaction speed demands. Increase the distance to challenge acceleration and running power.
step.task: Add a turn, jump, stepover or ball mastery skill before players can escape.
step.equipment: Add gates. Players score bonus points if they escape through a gate before being tagged.
step.people: Rotate partners regularly to expose children to different opponents and challenges.

base.showmanship: Build a rivalry between England and Scotland. Use famous players, countdowns, celebrations and commentary to create a tournament atmosphere. Adapt the countries to match current international fixtures where appropriate.
